title: Recover the Max Heap After Multiple Deletions

statement.markdown: Given a max heap represented as an array, implement a function to recover the heap after multiple deletions of elements. The deleted elements may not be at the end of the array.
statement.inputFormat: An integer array representing the max heap, where index i represents the value at the i-th index in the array.
statement.outputFormat: The recovered max heap represented as an array, where the heap property is maintained.
statement.notes: When recovering the heap, the deleted elements should be restored to their correct positions, and all other elements should be in their heap property.

examples.explanation: Delete element 3, recover max heap property
Delete element 9, recover max heap property

hints.text: Understand the problem, max heap property, and deletion strategy
Rebuild heap after deletions by applying heapify

editorial.title: Recover the Max Heap After Multiple Deletions Editorial
editorial.intuition: Use a binary heap data structure to efficiently recover the heap after deletions
editorial.bruteForce: Recreate the heap from scratch for each deletion
editorial.betterSolution: Use a priority queue to efficiently recover the heap
editorial.optimalSolution: Heapify the array after each deletion
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The recovered heap satisfies the max heap property
editorial.dryRun: Delete and recover a small max heap to understand the process
commonMistakes: Incorrectly rebuilding the heap after deletions

starterCode.java: class Solution { public void recoverMaxHeap(int[] arr) { // TODO: implement } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def recoverMaxHeap(self, nums: list[int]) -> None: pass # TODO: implement

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: void recoverMaxHeap(vector<int>& arr) { int i = 0; for (int j = 0; j < arr.size(); j++) if (j % 2 == 0) arr[i++] = arr[j]; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public void recoverMaxHeap(int[] arr) { int i = 0; for (int j = 0; j < arr.length; j++) if (j % 2 == 0) arr[i++] = arr[j]; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def recoverMaxHeap(self, nums: list[int]) -> None: i = 0; for j in range(len(nums)): if j % 2 == 0: nums[i] = nums[j]; i += 1
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function recoverMaxHeap(nums) { var i = 0; for (var j = 0; j < nums.length; j++) if (j % 2 == 0) nums[i++] = nums[j]; }
